title: Recursion & Backtracking Mastery
description: Master the art of thinking recursively! From simple recursion to complex backtracking problems. Learn how to break problems down and conquer them step by step. 🔄

documentation.content:
# Recursion & Backtracking Mastery

Recursion is one of the most beautiful concepts in computer science. Instead of thinking iteratively (loop, loop, loop), you think recursively: How can I break this problem into smaller versions of itself?

## The Recursive Leap of Faith

Imagine you're asked to find a book in a library. You don't search every shelf yourself. Instead, you ask a librarian Find the book, and they ask another librarian Find the book, and so on... until someone finds it. That's recursion!

## Three Golden Rules of Recursion

1. Base Case: The Exit - Every recursive function MUST have a base case - a condition where the function stops calling itself and returns a direct answer.

2. Recursive Case: The Breakdown - This is where you call the function on a smaller version of the problem. Each call should move closer to the base case.

3. Trust the Process - When you write the recursive case, assume that the recursive call will work correctly.

## Classic Recursion Patterns

Pattern 1: Linear Recursion - The function calls itself once.

Pattern 2: Tree Recursion - The function calls itself multiple times, creating a tree-like structure.

Pattern 3: Tail Recursion - The recursive call is the last operation. Smart compilers can optimize this!

submodules.title: Basic Recursion
submodules.description: Start with the fundamentals: factorial, fibonacci, and power functions

submodules.title: Tree Recursion
submodules.description: Master fibonacci, tree problems, and multiple recursive calls

submodules.title: Backtracking Algorithms
submodules.description: Learn to solve N-Queens, Sudoku, and permutation problems

problems.title: Factorial
problems.description: Calculate n! using recursion. Write a recursive function to calculate the factorial of a number n.

problems.title: Fibonacci Number
problems.description: Find the nth Fibonacci number. Write a recursive function with memoization for efficiency.

problems.title: Power(x, n)
problems.description: Calculate x raised to power n using recursion efficiently. Note: n can be negative and very large.

problems.title: N-Queens Problem
problems.description: Place N queens on an NxN chessboard such that no two queens can attack each other. Return all valid configurations.

problems.title: Generate Parentheses
problems.description: Given n pairs of parentheses, generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.

problems.title: Letter Combinations of Phone
problems.description: Given a string containing digits 2-9, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent on a phone keypad.

problems.title: Permutations
problems.description: Given an array of distinct integers, return all possible permutations using backtracking.

problems.title: Word Search
problems.description: Given an m x n grid of characters and a string word, determine if the word exists in the grid using backtracking.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a base case in recursion?
options: To make the code run faster
To determine when the recursion should stop and return a value
To initialize variables
To handle errors
questions.explanation: The base case is crucial - it's the condition that stops the recursion and returns a value directly without making another recursive call. Without it, you get infinite recursion.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of naive recursive Fibonacci without memoization?
options: O(n)
O(n log n)
O(log n)
questions.explanation: Without memoization, fibonacci recalculates the same values many times, leading to exponential time complexity O(2^n). With memoization, it becomes O(n).

questions.question: What does backtracking mean in recursion?
options: Going back to the previous function call
Undoing choices and trying a different path when current path doesnt lead to a solution
Repeating the recursion from the start
Storing previous function calls in memory
questions.explanation: Backtracking is when you explore a path, and if it doesn't work out, you undo your choices and try a different path. This is crucial for problems like N-Queens and Sudoku.

questions.question: What is tail recursion?
options: Recursion at the end of a function
When the last operation in a recursive function is the recursive call itself
When multiple functions call each other recursively
Recursion that ends with a return statement
questions.explanation: Tail recursion is when the recursive call is the very last operation in the function. Smart compilers can optimize tail recursion to use iteration instead, saving stack space.

questions.question: What problem can occur with very deep recursion?
options: Infinite loops
Stack overflow - running out of call stack space
Memory leak
CPU overheating
questions.explanation: Each recursive call creates a new stack frame. With very deep recursion, you run out of stack space and get a stack overflow error.
